auth.usage_instructions: Use `buttondown auth` for manage authentication. Available subcommands here are `login`, `logout`, `status`, `test`, `profiles`.

login.help: Configure authentication credentials. Prompts for required credentials based on the tool's authentication type. For OAuth authorization code flows, opens a browser for user consent.
login.usage_instructions: Run `buttondown auth login` to configure authentication credentials. Prompts for required credentials based on the tool's authentication type. For OAuth authorization code flows, opens a browser for user consent. Common options: `--profile/-p`, `--force/-F`, `--credential-type/-c`.

options.name: --profile
options.help: Profile name to save credentials to

logout.usage_instructions: Run `buttondown auth logout` to clear stored credentials. Common options: `--profile/-p`.

options.name: --profile
options.help: Profile name to clear credentials from

profiles.usage_instructions: Use `buttondown auth profiles` for manage authentication profiles. Available subcommands here are `list`, `get`, `create`, `set-default`, `delete`.

create.help: Create a new profile from .env.example template.
create.usage_instructions: Run `buttondown auth profiles create` to create a new profile from .env.example template. Required arguments: `NAME`.

delete.help: Delete a profile and its data.
delete.usage_instructions: Run `buttondown auth profiles delete` to delete a profile and its data. Required arguments: `NAME`. Common options: `--force/-F`.

get.help: Get details for a specific profile.
get.usage_instructions: Run `buttondown auth profiles get` to get details for a specific profile. Required arguments: `NAME`. Common options: `--table/-t`.

arguments.name: name
arguments.help: Profile name to get details for

list.help: List all profiles and show which is the default.
list.usage_instructions: Run `buttondown auth profiles list` to list all profiles and show which is the default. Common options: `--table/-t`, `--limit/-l`, `--filter/-f`, `--properties/-p`.

options.name: --limit
options.help: Maximum number of profiles to return

options.name: --properties
options.help: Comma-separated list of fields to include

remove.help: Delete a profile and its data.

select.help: Activate a profile within its auth type.

arguments.name: name
arguments.help: Profile name to activate within its auth type

status.help: Check authentication status across profiles. Performs a live round-trip for every configured credential type so the report reflects ground truth — not on-disk belief. Saved credentials whose live verification fails are reported as ``authenticated: false`` with the failure reason in ``api_test``.
status.usage_instructions: Run `buttondown auth status` to check authentication status across profiles. Performs a live round-trip for every configured credential type so the report reflects ground truth — not on-disk belief. Saved credentials whose live verification fails are reported as ``authenticated: false`` with the failure reason in ``api_test``. Common options: `--table/-t`, `--profile/-p`.

options.name: --profile
options.help: Profile name to check (defaults to all profiles)

test.help: Test authentication by verifying credentials work across profiles.
test.usage_instructions: Run `buttondown auth test` to test authentication by verifying credentials work across profiles. Common options: `--table/-t`, `--profile/-p`.

automations.usage_instructions: Use `buttondown automations` for manage automations. Available subcommands here are `list`, `get`, `create`, `update`, `delete`.

create.usage_instructions: Run `buttondown automations create` to create an automation. Common options: `--table/-t`, `--properties/-p`.

options.name: --actions-file
options.help: Read actions JSON array from file

delete.usage_instructions: Run `buttondown automations delete` to delete an automation. Required arguments: `AUTOMATION_ID`. Common options: `--table/-t`, `--force/-F`.

get.usage_instructions: Run `buttondown automations get` to get a single automation. Required arguments: `AUTOMATION_ID`. Common options: `--table/-t`, `--properties/-p`.

list.usage_instructions: Run `buttondown automations list` to list automations. Common options: `--table/-t`, `--limit/-l`, `--filter/-f`, `--properties/-p`.

update.usage_instructions: Run `buttondown automations update` to update an automation. Required arguments: `AUTOMATION_ID`. Common options: `--table/-t`, `--properties/-p`.

options.name: --actions-file
options.help: Read actions JSON array from file

cache.usage_instructions: Use `buttondown cache` for manage response cache. Available subcommands here are `clear`.

clear.help: Remove all cached responses. Deletes every file in the cache directory ({storage_dir}/cache/) and reports how many files and bytes were freed.
clear.usage_instructions: Run `buttondown cache clear` to remove all cached responses. Deletes every file in the cache directory ({storage_dir}/cache/) and reports how many files and bytes were freed.

emails.usage_instructions: Use `buttondown emails` for manage emails. Available subcommands here are `list`, `get`, `create`, `update`, `delete`, `send-draft`, `render`.

create.usage_instructions: Run `buttondown emails create` to create an email. Common options: `--table/-t`, `--properties/-p`.

delete.usage_instructions: Run `buttondown emails delete` to delete an email. Required arguments: `EMAIL_ID`. Common options: `--table/-t`, `--force/-F`.

get.usage_instructions: Run `buttondown emails get` to get an email. Required arguments: `EMAIL_ID`. Common options: `--table/-t`, `--properties/-p`.

list.usage_instructions: Run `buttondown emails list` to list emails. Common options: `--table/-t`, `--limit/-l`, `--filter/-f`, `--properties/-p`.

render.help: Retrieve the rendered HTML of an email from the Buttondown API.
render.usage_instructions: Run `buttondown emails render` to retrieve the rendered HTML of an email from the Buttondown API. Required arguments: `EMAIL_ID`. Options: `--target`, `--output/-o`, `--open`.

options.name: --target
options.help: Render target: 'email' (inlined for inbox) or 'html' (web archive chrome)

options.name: --output
options.help: Write rendered HTML to this file instead of stdout

options.name: --open
options.help: Open the rendered HTML in the default browser

send-draft.usage_instructions: Run `buttondown emails send-draft` to send a draft email. Required arguments: `EMAIL_ID`. Common options: `--table/-t`.

update.usage_instructions: Run `buttondown emails update` to update an email. Required arguments: `EMAIL_ID`. Common options: `--table/-t`, `--properties/-p`.

feeds.usage_instructions: Use `buttondown feeds` for manage rss-to-email external feeds. Available subcommands here are `list`, `get`, `create`, `update`, `delete`.

create.usage_instructions: Run `buttondown feeds create` to create a new RSS feed. Common options: `--table/-t`, `--properties/-p`.

delete.usage_instructions: Run `buttondown feeds delete` to delete an RSS feed. Required arguments: `FEED_ID`. Common options: `--table/-t`, `--force/-F`.

get.usage_instructions: Run `buttondown feeds get` to get a single RSS feed. Required arguments: `FEED_ID`. Common options: `--table/-t`, `--properties/-p`.

list.usage_instructions: Run `buttondown feeds list` to list external RSS feeds. Common options: `--table/-t`, `--limit/-l`, `--filter/-f`, `--properties/-p`.

update.usage_instructions: Run `buttondown feeds update` to update an existing RSS feed. Required arguments: `FEED_ID`. Common options: `--table/-t`, `--properties/-p`.

subscribers.usage_instructions: Use `buttondown subscribers` for manage subscribers. Available subcommands here are `list`, `get`, `create`, `update`, `delete`, `send-link`, `remind`.

create.usage_instructions: Run `buttondown subscribers create` to create a subscriber. Common options: `--table/-t`, `--properties/-p`.

delete.usage_instructions: Run `buttondown subscribers delete` to delete a subscriber. Required arguments: `ID_OR_EMAIL`. Common options: `--table/-t`, `--force/-F`.

get.usage_instructions: Run `buttondown subscribers get` to get a subscriber. Required arguments: `ID_OR_EMAIL`. Common options: `--table/-t`, `--properties/-p`.

list.usage_instructions: Run `buttondown subscribers list` to list subscribers. Common options: `--table/-t`, `--limit/-l`, `--filter/-f`, `--properties/-p`.

remind.usage_instructions: Run `buttondown subscribers remind` to send a subscriber reminder. Required arguments: `ID_OR_EMAIL`. Common options: `--table/-t`.

send-link.usage_instructions: Run `buttondown subscribers send-link` to send a subscriber magic link. Required arguments: `ID_OR_EMAIL`. Common options: `--table/-t`.

update.usage_instructions: Run `buttondown subscribers update` to update a subscriber. Required arguments: `ID_OR_EMAIL`. Common options: `--table/-t`, `--properties/-p`.

tags.usage_instructions: Use `buttondown tags` for manage tags. Available subcommands here are `list`, `get`, `create`, `update`, `delete`, `analytics`.

analytics.usage_instructions: Run `buttondown tags analytics` to get tag analytics. Required arguments: `TAG_ID`. Common options: `--table/-t`, `--properties/-p`.

create.usage_instructions: Run `buttondown tags create` to create a tag. Common options: `--table/-t`, `--properties/-p`.

delete.usage_instructions: Run `buttondown tags delete` to delete a tag. Required arguments: `TAG_ID`. Common options: `--table/-t`, `--force/-F`.

get.usage_instructions: Run `buttondown tags get` to get a tag. Required arguments: `TAG_ID`. Common options: `--table/-t`, `--properties/-p`.

list.usage_instructions: Run `buttondown tags list` to list tags. Common options: `--table/-t`, `--limit/-l`, `--filter/-f`, `--properties/-p`.

update.usage_instructions: Run `buttondown tags update` to update a tag. Required arguments: `TAG_ID`. Common options: `--table/-t`, `--properties/-p`.

usage_instructions: Use for cli interface for buttondown api. Commands follow `buttondown <group> <action>`. Primary groups here are `buttondown auth`, `buttondown cache`, `buttondown automations`, `buttondown emails`, `buttondown feeds`, `buttondown subscribers`, `buttondown tags`.
